overview: A leave management system with a React frontend (web-app) that calls a Go backend API (service) for leave request operations. The backend exposes RESTful endpoints for submitting, approving, and querying leave requests, plus checking balances. Communication is synchronous HTTP/JSON between the web-app and the API.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests specifying start date, end date, and leave type
Managers must be able to view pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests with optional comments
Employees must be able to view the status of their own leave requests
All users must be able to check their remaining leave balance by leave type
The system must distinguish between employee and manager roles
Leave requests must track status: pending, approved, or rejected
The system must calculate leave days from start and end dates
All components must expose a /health endpoint for readiness checks

components.name: leave-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend SPA for leave management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Build a React single-page application in TypeScript. Use Create React App or Vite. Serve on port 3000. Implement a health endpoint at GET /health returning {"status":"ok"}. Create pages: (1) Leave Request Form — date pickers for start/end dates, dropdown for leave type (Annual, Sick, Casual), submit button calling POST /leave-api/requests. (2) My Requests — table showing user's requests with columns: dates, type, status, manager comment. Fetch from GET /leave-api/requests?employee={userId}. (3) Manager Dashboard — table of pending requests from team with Approve/Reject buttons calling PATCH /leave-api/requests/{id}. Fetch from GET /leave-api/requests?status=pending&manager={userId}. (4) Leave Balance — display remaining days per leave type from GET /leave-api/balance. Use React Router for navigation. Mock user authentication by storing userId and role (employee/manager) in sessionStorage. Show/hide Manager Dashboard based on role. Use fetch or axios for API calls to leave-api service. Handle errors with user-friendly messages. Use basic styling with CSS or a UI library like Material-UI. Dockerfile: multi-stage build with Node 18, npm install, npm run build, serve with nginx:alpine copying build to /usr/share/nginx/html. Expose port 3000 via nginx config.

components.name: leave-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend API for leave request management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /requests:
    get:
      summary: List leave requests
      parameters:
        - name: employee
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Filter by employee ID
        - name: manager
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Filter by manager ID (shows team requests)
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
          description: Filter by status
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
    post:
      summary: Submit a leave request
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [employeeId, startDate, endDate, leaveType]
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [Annual, Sick, Casual]
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
  /requests/{id}:
    patch:
      summary: Approve or reject a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [status, managerId]
              properties:
                status:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approved, rejected]
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comment:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Request not found
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
  /balance:
    get:
      summary: Get leave balance for a user
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  employeeId:
                    type: string
                  balances:
                    type: object
                    additionalProperties:
                      type: number
                    example:
                      Annual: 15
                      Sick: 10
                      Casual: 5
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        managerId:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        leaveType:
          type: string
          enum: [Annual, Sick, Casual]
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        comment:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Build a Go HTTP service using net/http. Listen on port 9090. Implement GET /health returning JSON {"status":"ok"}. Use in-memory data structures (maps, slices) for storage: a slice of LeaveRequest structs and a map of employee balances. LeaveRequest struct: ID (string, UUID), EmployeeID (string), ManagerID (string), StartDate (time.Time), EndDate (time.Time), LeaveType (string: Annual/Sick/Casual), Status (string: pending/approved/rejected), Comment (string), CreatedAt (time.Time), UpdatedAt (time.Time). Initialize with sample data: 3 employees with IDs emp1, emp2, emp3; manager IDs mgr1, mgr2; balances map with Annual:15, Sick:10, Casual:5 per employee. Seed 2-3 pending requests. POST /requests: parse JSON body, validate required fields and date range (endDate >= startDate), generate UUID, set status=pending, store, return 201 with created object. GET /requests: support query params employee, manager, status; filter in-memory slice; manager filter returns requests where request.ManagerID equals query param; return filtered array. PATCH /requests/{id}: find by ID, validate status is approved or rejected, update Status, ManagerID, Comment, UpdatedAt; return 200 with updated object or 404 if not found. GET /balance?employeeId=X: return JSON object with employeeId and balances map. Use encoding/json for marshaling. Enable CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET,POST,PATCH,OPTIONS, Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type). Handle OPTIONS preflight. Use http.HandleFunc for routing or a lightweight router like gorilla/mux. Log requests to stdout. Dockerfile: FROM golang:1.21-alpine AS builder, WORKDIR /app, COPY go.mod go.sum ./, RUN go mod download, COPY . ., RUN go build -o leave-api .; FROM alpine:latest, COPY --from=builder /app/leave-api /leave-api, EXPOSE 9090, CMD ["/leave-api"]. Ensure go.mod module name is leave-api. Use time.Parse for date parsing. Return appropriate HTTP status codes and error messages for validation failures.
